what_it_does: An IIFE that exposes window.forkBrainCore, providing a single shared brain-turn streamer (talk) that posts to /api/claude/turn and parses the NDJSON reply, plus a batched route-back writer (writeDirections) that posts to /api/territory/write. It keeps per-address session continuity, strips markdown from displayed text, maps opaque pointable tokens to local addresses, and suppresses writes when the URL contains ?verify.
observations: Per-address session IDs are stored in _sessions and reused on subsequent turns.
The NDJSON stream is parsed manually with a TextDecoder and buffered by newlines.
Markdown syntax is stripped from every text emit as a display-layer safety net.
Pointable tokens are mapped to addresses client-side; only token+label are sent to the server.
writeDirections groups items by element_id and emits gallery:rerender on success or gallery:write-error on failure.
In ?verify mode, writes are suppressed with console warnings and gallery:write-suppressed events.
The module is intended to be mounted by both a per-gallery-unit brain hook and the global V/RHM brain.
standouts: The verification guard is placed at the single write chokepoint, not duplicated in callers.
Markdown stripping is applied uniformly to both the default reply element and any custom onText renderer.
Pointable addresses never leave the client; the server receives only opaque tokens and labels.
conventions: Uses an IIFE to avoid polluting scope and attaches the public API to window.
Uses fetch + getReader + TextDecoder for streaming NDJSON.
Communicates with the rest of the app via CustomEvent dispatches.
Fail-loud: errors are surfaced to the UI and via events rather than swallowed.
concerns: _sessions may grow unbounded if addresses are never evicted.
The markdown stripper is regex-based and may mishandle nested or edge-case markup.
The streaming reader does not check resp.ok before attempting to read the body.
_verifyMode only inspects the URL query string; other verification signals are ignored.
If replyEl is null and opts.onText is absent, setText becomes a no-op and '(no reply)' is not visible.
notes: Central shared substrate for Claude turns and territory writes, with a verification guard and markdown normalization at the display seam.
questions: What is the exact NDJSON event schema returned by /api/claude/turn?
How and when is window.surfacePointables populated and refreshed?
What is the expected item shape for /api/territory/write?
Is there any session eviction or reset mechanism for _sessions?
How does the V mount resolve the current aim address before calling talk()?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a single reusable brain-turn engine with per-address continuity, pointable dispatch, and a guarded route-back writer.
intention_signals: Consolidate two brain mounts into one reusable turn-and-write substrate while preventing automated verification drives from persisting data and ensuring the operator always sees clean, markdown-free prose.
novelty: Combines a shared NDJSON Claude turn parser with local pointable token mapping and a URL-guarded, event-emitting territory-write chokepoint in one file.
contribution: Provides a reusable, address-scoped Claude turn streamer and a batched, observable, verification-aware territory write dispatcher that both gallery and V brain mounts can share without reimplementing session continuity, markdown stripping, or write guards.
summary_for_embedding: An IIFE exposing window.forkBrainCore with talk() for NDJSON Claude turns and writeDirections() for batched territory writes, including per-address session continuity, markdown stripping, pointable dispatch, and URL-based verification suppression.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the single shared brain-turn and route-back substrate for both gallery-unit and V/RHM brain interfaces.

symbols.does: Strips common markdown syntax from a string so the rendered output is plain prose.
symbols.description: A regex-based normalizer that removes code fences, inline code, bold, italic, headers, and bullet markers from text. It is applied to every text emit so the spoken RHM voice never displays literal markdown characters.

symbols.does: Streams a Claude turn from /api/claude/turn and renders the reply while managing session continuity and pointables.
symbols.description: An async function that POSTs a prompt and address to /api/claude/turn, reads the NDJSON response, accumulates text deltas, applies markdown stripping, handles tool/point/done/error events, maintains per-address session IDs, dispatches ui:point for recognized pointable tokens, and invokes optional callbacks.

symbols.does: Wraps the text emitter with markdown stripping before rendering.
symbols.description: A local arrow function that calls _emit with the markdown-stripped version of the accumulated text, ensuring both the default reply element and any custom onText callback receive clean prose.

symbols.does: Toggles a loading class on the reply element during the wait state.
symbols.description: A local arrow function that adds or removes the 'brain-loading' CSS class on replyEl while the brain is waiting for the first token, but only when no custom onText renderer is provided.

symbols.does: Detects verification mode from the URL query string.
symbols.description: A helper that returns true when window.location.search contains a 'verify' parameter, used by writeDirections to decide whether to suppress persistence.

symbols.does: Batches direction items by element_id and posts them to /api/territory/write.
symbols.description: A function that groups direction items per element_id, sends each group to /api/territory/write, emits gallery:rerender on success, and emits gallery:write-error on HTTP or network failure. It suppresses all writes and emits gallery:write-suppressed events when ?verify is present.
